criteria.label: State MSE, MAE, BCE, and one-hot CE formulas and compute each on a toy example.
criteria.example: Given y=4, ŷ=1 compute MSE=9 and MAE=3. Given y=1, ŷ=0.2 compute BCE=−log(0.2). Given class 1 with softmax [0.2, 0.8] compute CE=−log(0.8).

criteria.label: Pick the correct loss for a described supervised problem (regression, binary, multi-class).
criteria.example: House price → MSE or MAE. Spam yes/no → BCE. 10-digit MNIST → CE. Explain why MSE on one-hot digits is wrong.

criteria.label: Explain how BCE/CE connect to H(P, Q) without re-deriving Shannon entropy.
criteria.example: Point to Cross-entropy topic for H(P, Q) = −Σ p log q; show BCE is Bernoulli H(P,Q) and one-hot CE is −log q(y).

criteria.label: Distinguish training loss from evaluation metrics (MSE vs RMSE, CE vs accuracy).
criteria.example: Train with MSE; report test RMSE. Train with CE; report test log loss and accuracy separately — same model, different reporting roles.
